title: eryngium dubium
intro: Eryngium dubium, also known as the sea lavender or prickly chervil, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant's flowers, stems, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eryngium dubium include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Eryngium dubium can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
